text: How did Hawkwood make money in times of peace?
zh: 霍克伍德在和平时期是如何赚钱的？

text: There was a time when the owners of shops and businesses in Chicago had to pay large sums of money to gangsters in return for 'protection'.
zh: 曾几何时，芝加哥的店主和商人不得不向歹徒交纳巨额金钱以换取'保护'。

text: If the money was not paid promptly, the gangsters would quickly put a man out of business by destroying his shop.
zh: 如果不及时付钱，歹徒就会迅速捣毁他的店铺，使他破产。

text: Obtaining 'protection money' is not a modern crime.
zh: 收取'保护费'并非现代才有的罪行。

text: As long ago as the fourteenth century, an Englishman, Sir John Hawkwood, made the remarkable discovery that people would rather pay large sums of money than have their life work destroyed by gangsters.
zh: 早在14世纪，英国人约翰·霍克伍德爵士就有了一个惊人的发现：人们宁愿付出巨款，也不愿眼睁睁看着自己毕生的心血被歹徒摧毁。

text: Six hundred years ago, Sir John Hawkwood arrived in Italy with a band of soldiers and settled near Florence.
zh: 六百年前，约翰·霍克伍德爵士带着一队士兵来到意大利，在佛罗伦萨附近安顿下来。

text: He soon made a name for himself and came to be known to the Italians as Giovanni Acuto.
zh: 他很快就闯出了名声，意大利人称他为乔瓦尼·阿库托。

text: Whenever the Italian city-states were at war with each other, Hawkwood used to hire his soldiers to princes who were willing to pay the high price he demanded.
zh: 每当意大利各城邦互相交战时，霍克伍德就把他的士兵租借给愿意支付他所要求的高价的君主。

text: In times of peace, when business was bad, Hawkwood and his men would march into a city-state, and after burning down a few farms, would offer to go away if protection money was paid to them.
zh: 在和平时期，生意清淡时，霍克伍德和他的手下就会开进某个城邦，烧毁几处农庄后，提出只要对方交纳保护费，他们便会撤走。

text: Hawkwood made large sums of money in this way.
zh: 霍克伍德就这样赚了大笔钱财。

text: In spite of this, the Italians regarded him as a sort of hero.
zh: 尽管如此，意大利人仍把他视为一种英雄人物。

text: When he died at the age of eighty, the Florentines gave him a state funeral and had a picture painted which was dedicated to the memory of 'the most valiant soldier and most notable leader, Signor Giovanni Haukodue'.
zh: 他八十岁去世时，佛罗伦萨人为他举行了国葬，还请人画了一幅画像，题词纪念'最英勇的战士、最杰出的统帅乔瓦尼·霍克德先生'。
